title: DRPL/1 — Deep Research Pipeline Language document
description: A declarative description of a deep-research pipeline's STRUCTURE: phases, dataflow, failure contracts, model routing, and privacy placement (which party executes each phase and which parties receive which data). Normative prose: docs/PIPELINE-LANGUAGE.md; reference tooling: sdk/drpl.mjs (validate / fingerprint / diff). This schema mirrors validateDrpl — the tooling is authoritative where they disagree.

title.description: Prose — never part of any structural level.

meta.description: Free annotation container — never part of any structural level.

vocabTerm.description: A registered term, or an x-… extension term.

id.description: Unique within the document. Structurally IGNORED (canonicalization replaces ids with topological positions); used only for diff alignment and display.

kind.description: Registered kinds: triage, enrichment, recall, search, notes, gap-check, synthesis, validation, tool-loop, transform, grade, human-gate. Anything else must be x-….

needs.description: Phase ids this phase consumes — the dataflow DAG (cycles are invalid).

optional.description: True for phases the pipeline runs only when configured/available; the --spine projection drops them (rewiring needs transitively) to expose the required research spine.

repeats.description: Whether this phase (and its needs subgraph) may re-run in a bounded loop. Repeatability is shape-level; the bound is full-level.

at.description: Which tier executes the phase's orchestration.

calls.description: Which parties RECEIVE DATA during this phase, and exactly what crosses the wire — the minimal-outbound rule made declarable. An empty/absent list asserts no data leaves the executing tier.

party.description: Registered parties: origin-server, model-provider, search-provider, embedding-provider, enrichment-provider, self-hosted. Anything else must be x-….

carries.description: What data crosses to this party (open vocabulary: question, conversation, search-queries, coordinates, host, source-digest, …).

model.description: Present when the phase calls an LLM.

route.description: Split model routing: planning phases pin to the fixed reliable model; only answer phases run on the user's chosen model.

tools.description: false = deterministic, no function calling (the default posture). A tool list declares an agentic tool loop explicitly.

policy.description: soft: the phase degrades to a lesser result and the pipeline continues; hard: the phase failing fails the request.

degradesTo.description: Prose description of the degraded result — structurally ignored.

emits.description: Optional labels for what the phase produces (full-level).
